theme: wiring a sensor mesh to prescribed electrical distances
title: Resistance Portrait: Fitting a Sensor Mesh to Prescribed Electrical Distances
strategies: trivial: uniform conductance-1 backbone path over the active terminals, ignoring targets (reproduces the checker baseline)
greedy: light backbone plus a direct wire of conductance 1/target for the most important pairs -- overshoots because every wire lowers all resistances (Rayleigh monotonicity)

strong: damped fixed-point on the coupled pseudoinverse -- recompute ALL pairwise resistances and multiplicatively correct each shared wire toward its residual, keeping the best iterate by exact error
advanced: joint least-squares / Newton over the conductance vector with edge-budget allocation that reinforces the backbone shared by many targets rather than fitting pairs one at a time
